segments.text: When Isaac was old and his eyes were so weak that he could no longer see, he called his older son Esau and said to him,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: My son.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Here I am,
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Esau replied.
Look,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: said Isaac,
I am now old, and I do not know the day of my death.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Take your weapons—your quiver and bow—and go out into the field to hunt some game for me.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Then prepare a tasty dish that I love and bring it to me to eat, so that I may bless you before I die.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Now Rebekah was listening to what Isaac told his son Esau. So when Esau went into the field to hunt game and bring it back,
Rebekah said to her son Jacob,
Behold, I overheard your father saying to your brother Esau,
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: ‘Bring me some game and prepare me a tasty dish to eat, so that I may bless you in the presence of the LORD before I die.’
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Now, my son, listen to my voice and do exactly as I tell you.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Go out to the flock and bring me two choice young goats, so that I can make them into a tasty dish for your father—the kind he loves.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Then take it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before he dies.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Jacob answered his mother Rebekah,
Look, my brother Esau is a hairy man, but I am smooth-skinned.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: What if my father touches me? Then I would be revealed to him as a deceiver, and I would bring upon myself a curse rather than a blessing.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: His mother replied,
Your curse be on me, my son. Just obey my voice and go get them for me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: So Jacob went and got two goats and brought them to his mother, who made the tasty food his father loved.
And Rebekah took the finest clothes in the house that belonged to her older son Esau, and she put them on her younger son Jacob.
She also put the skins of the young goats on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.
Then she handed her son Jacob the tasty food and bread she had made.
So Jacob went to his father and said,
My father.
segments.speaker: Jacob went to his father and

segments.text: Here I am!
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: he answered.
Which one are you, my son?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Jacob said to his father,
I am Esau, your firstborn. I have done as you told me. Please sit up and eat some of my game, so that you may bless me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: But Isaac asked his son,
How did you ever find it so quickly, my son?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Because the LORD your God brought it to me,
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: he replied.
Then Isaac said to Jacob,
Please come closer so I can touch you, my son. Are you really my son Esau, or not?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: So Jacob came close to his father Isaac, who touched him and said,
The voice is the voice of Jacob, but the hands are the hands of Esau.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Isaac did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like those of his brother Esau; so he blessed him.
Again he asked,
Are you really my son Esau?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: And he replied,
I am.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Serve me,
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: said Isaac,
and let me eat some of my son’s game, so that I may bless you.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Jacob brought it to him, and he ate; then he brought him wine, and he drank.
Then his father Isaac said to him,
Please come near and kiss me, my son.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: So he came near and kissed him. When Isaac smelled his clothing, he blessed him and said:

Ah, the smell of my sonis like the smell of a field
that the LORD has blessed.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text:
May God give to you the dew of heaven and the richness of the earth—
an abundance of grain and new wine.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: May peoples serve you and nations bow down to you. May you be the master of your brothers, and may the sons of your mother bow down to you. May those who curse you be cursed, and those who bless you be blessed.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing him and Jacob had left his father’s presence, his brother Esau returned from the hunt.
He too made some tasty food, brought it to his father, and said to him,
My father, sit up and eat of your son’s game, so that you may bless me.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: But his father Isaac replied,
Who are you?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: I am Esau, your firstborn son,
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: he answered.
Isaac began to tremble violently and said,
Who was it, then, who hunted the game and brought it to me? Before you came in, I ate it all and blessed him—and indeed, he will be blessed!
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: When Esau heard his father’s words, he let out a loud and bitter cry and said to his father,
Bless me too, O my father!
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: But Isaac replied,
Your brother came deceitfully and took your blessing.
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: So Esau declared,
Is he not rightly named Jacob? For he has cheated me twice. He took my birthright, and now he has taken my blessing.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Then he asked,
Haven’t you saved a blessing for me?
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: But Isaac answered Esau:
Look, I have made him your master and given him all his relatives as servants; I have sustained him with grain and new wine. What is left that I can do for you, my son?
segments.speaker: Isaac

segments.text: Esau said to his father,
Do you have only one blessing, my father? Bless me too, O my father!
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Then Esau wept aloud.
His father Isaac answered him:

Behold, your dwelling place shall beaway from the richness of the land,
away from the dew of heaven above.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: You shall live by the sword and serve your brother. But when you rebel, you will tear his yoke from your neck.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: Esau held a grudge against Jacob because of the blessing his father had given him. And Esau said in his heart,
The days of mourning for my father are at hand; then I will kill my brother Jacob.
segments.speaker: Esau

segments.text: When the words of her older son Esau were relayed to Rebekah, she sent for her younger son Jacob and told him,
Look, your brother Esau is consoling himself by plotting to kill you.
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: So now, my son, obey my voice and flee at once to my brother Laban in Haran.
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: Stay with him for a while, until your brother’s fury subsides—
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: until your brother’s rage against you wanes and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send for you and bring you back from there. Why should I lose both of you in one day?
segments.speaker: the Word

segments.text: Then Rebekah said to Isaac,
I am weary of my life because of these Hittite women. If Jacob takes a Hittite wife from among them, what good is my life?
segments.speaker: Rebekah
